heroHeadline: Iran Submits Hormuz Reopening Proposal; Araghchi Meets Putin in Moscow — Dual Blockade Enters Day 59
heroSubtitle: Iran submitted a formal proposal to the United States on April 26 to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war — its most concrete peace overture since the conflict began — while deliberately deferring nuclear discussions to a later stage. Iranian FM Araghchi pressed the diplomatic offensive from Muscat (meeting Sultan Haitham) to Moscow (meeting Putin on Day 59), building international backing for Tehran's position. The US has not publicly responded; the IRGC simultaneously declared Hormuz control to be Iran's 'definitive strategy,' deepening the internal divide between Iran's diplomatic and military tracks. Bloomberg reported the dual blockade has halted commercial ship traffic through the strait for the first time in history — with Brent crude near $108/bbl — as global economic pressure intensifies and no resolution appears imminent.
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-27. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, IAEA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, NPR, CBS News, NBC News, Euronews, UN News, Axios, Washington Post, Army Recognition, France 24, SOF News, Pakistan Today, Iran Wire, Voice of Emirates, The New York Times, The Guardian, The Japan Times, Critical Threats, US News, House of Commons Library.
